title: [Luxray] Battle Guide
summary: A physical [Electric] [pivot] defined by [intimidate] on entry, [wild-charge] and [crunch] damage, and [volt-switch] escape routes de[spite] only base 70 Speed.

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Luxray] earns its slot by being one of the only physical [Electric] attackers in the format that carries [intimidate] on entry. The physical [Electric] niche is thin, and almost every other option lacks a way to shave the opponent's Attack for free. Base 120 Atk and 70 Speed are awkward on their own, but [intimidate] plus [volt-switch] [pivot] play turn those numbers into real value.

## Strengths
STAB [wild-charge] from 120 Atk with a Jolly nature puts [Luxray] at the top of the physical [Electric] damage chart even without a damage-boosting item. [crunch] breaks [Ghost] and [Psychic] walls while also catching [Grass] and [Electric]-resist [pivot]s that would otherwise stall it out. The real tool is [ice-fang], which turns the usual [Dragon]/[Ground]/[Flying] answer list into a clean 4x cove[rage] chart. Dropping in mid-turn with [intimidate] lets [Luxray] blunt an incoming physical hit and then [volt-switch] out into the correct [counter] on your side.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[Luxray] is 2x weak to [Ground] and has a shallow 70 base Speed, so any Scarf user or priority physical threat forces it into trades it does not want. Physical [Ground] walls such as Hippowdon-style tanks eat everything [Luxray] has outside of [ice-fang] and hit back for huge damage. [wild-charge] re[coil] is the silent killer in long matches — stacked re[coil] plus [stealth-rock] chip will kill [Luxray] before a second setup window ever opens.

## Recommended Moveset
[wild-charge] is the locked main STAB, but treat it like a kill-confirm button rather than a spam move — re[coil] is the biggest HP drain in its set. [crunch] is the secondary STAB-adjacent slot that lets [Luxray] actually threaten [Ghost] and [Psychic] types and punish [Grass] [pivot]s that would normally come in for free on an [Electric]. [ice-fang] covers [Dragon], [Ground], and [Flying] all at once and is non-negotiable. The fourth slot is [volt-switch], which is what actually makes [Luxray] a [pivot] rather than a worse version of other physical [Electric]s. A [superpower] swap-in is the best alternative if the team lacks a reliable answer to [Normal], [Rock], and [Steel] walls.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[sitrus-berry] is the default item — it offsets one [wild-charge] re[coil] cycle and pushes [Luxray] over the HP threshold it needs to get a second attack off. [focus-sash] is the aggressive alternative that guarantees survival against one priority or Choice Scarf user and buys a free [volt-switch] turn. The ability is locked to [intimidate]; it is the entire reason to run [Luxray]. For Tera, [Electric] deepens [wild-charge] damage, while Tera [Ground] removes the 2x [Ground] weakness entirely and turns [Luxray] into a surprise defensive check.

## Teammates & Synergy
[Luxray] needs a [Grass] or [Flying] [pivot] to [absorb] the [Ground] attacks that would otherwise force it out — pair it with a slow U-turn partner so that [volt-switch] and U-turn form a forced-momentum loop. A special cleaner in the back capitalizes on the physical cushion [intimidate] creates. A rocks-remover is nice but not mandatory, since [Luxray] is not expected to switch in and out more than twice per match.

## Play Patterns
On turn one, prioritize [intimidate] entry over offense — drop the opponent's Attack before committing to a damage move. Press [wild-charge] only on kill-confirmed ranges, since repeated re[coil] is what usually ends [Luxray]'s game. Once HP dips below a third, [volt-switch] out and let the [pivot] chain do the cleanup work.
